text.title: testing-with-assistive-technology Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the testing-with-assistive-technology methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Config MUST declare three tiers — automated_ci, manual_release, at_user_quarterly — with cadence + responsible owner per tier. Skipping any tier requires documented justification.
rule.rationale: Automated tools alone leave 50%+ of issues undetected; manual + AT-user sessions surface the rest.

rule.statement: Automated scan (axe-core / pa11y / lighthouse) MUST run on every PR; build MUST fail on new violations.
rule.rationale: PR-time enforcement prevents regression accumulation; gating at release time is too late.

rule.statement: Manual screen-reader + keyboard sweep MUST run before each release. At least one VoiceOver + one NVDA pass on critical journeys.
rule.rationale: Screen-reader behaviour differs significantly; coverage must include both major engines.

rule.statement: An assistive-tech user (paid for time) MUST review the product at least quarterly. Findings MUST be filed with the same severity rubric as engineering bugs.
rule.rationale: Synthetic testing cannot replicate cognitive load + real-world AT configurations users develop.

rule.statement: Each a11y bug MUST include: WCAG SC reference (e.g., 1.4.3), repro snippet, suggested ARIA APG pattern, scope estimate (1 file / multi-file / cross-module).
rule.rationale: Structured reports halve engineering triage cost and accelerate fixes.
